steps.title: Isolate xyz on one side
steps.content: Subtract $\tfrac{1}{5xy}$ from both sides: $$xyz=\frac{1}{4yz}-\frac{1}{5xy}$$ Find the common denominator $20xy^2z$: $$xyz=\frac{5x-4z}{20xy^2z}$$

steps.title: Solve for y²
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $y^2$ and divide by $xz$: $$y^2=\frac{5x-4z}{20x^2z^2}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $\dfrac{5x-4z}{20x^2z^2}$ — this is $y^2$, not $y$. Incorrect.
  • B: $\sqrt{\dfrac{5x-4z}{20x^2z^2}}$ — matches exactly. That confirms B is correct.
  • C and D involve $4xz^2-5x^2-z$, which does not arise from the algebra. Incorrect.
